segments.text: Then Jonah prayed to Yahweh, his God, out of the fish’s belly.
I called because of my affliction to Yahweh. He answered me. Out of the belly of Sheol[^5] I cried. You heard my voice.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: For you threw me into the depths, in the heart of the seas. The flood was all around me. All your waves and your billows passed over me.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: I said, ‘I have been banished from your sight; yet I will look again toward your holy temple.’
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: The waters surrounded me, even to the soul. The deep was around me. The weeds were wrapped around my head.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text:
I went down to the bottoms of the mountains. The earth barred me in forever; 
yet you have brought my life up from the pit, Yahweh my God.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: “When my soul fainted within me, I remembered Yahweh. My prayer came in to you, into your holy temple.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: Those who regard vain idols forsake their own mercy.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text:
But I will sacrifice to you with the voice of thanksgiving. 
I will pay that which I have vowed. Salvation belongs to Yahweh.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: Then Yahweh spoke to the fish, and it vomited out Jonah on the dry land.
